info.name: Azure Databricks REST API
Clusters
Azure Databricks Create a New Cluster

items.docs: Creates a new Apache Spark cluster. Returns the ID of the newly created cluster. The cluster starts in a PENDING state and transitions to RUNNING when ready. Optionally, you can attach libraries to the cluster after creation.

info.name: Azure Databricks Edit a Cluster

items.docs: Edits the configuration of an existing cluster. The cluster must be in a RUNNING or TERMINATED state. If the cluster is running, it will be restarted with the new configuration.

info.name: Azure Databricks Start a Terminated Cluster

items.docs: Starts a terminated cluster given its ID. Works only for clusters in a TERMINATED state. Uses the last specified cluster configuration.

info.name: Azure Databricks Restart a Running Cluster

items.docs: Restarts a running cluster given its ID. The cluster must be in a RUNNING state.

info.name: Azure Databricks Terminate a Cluster

items.docs: Terminates a cluster given its ID. The cluster is removed from the running state but its configuration is preserved so it can be restarted. Use permanent-delete to fully remove a cluster.

info.name: Azure Databricks Permanently Delete a Cluster

items.docs: Permanently deletes a Spark cluster. If the cluster is running, it is terminated and its resources freed. The cluster is removed permanently and cannot be restarted.

info.name: Azure Databricks Get Cluster Information

params.name: cluster_id
params.description: The cluster about which to retrieve information

items.docs: Retrieves the information for a cluster given its identifier. Returns the current state, configuration, and metadata for the cluster.

info.name: Azure Databricks List All Clusters

params.name: can_use_client
params.description: Filter clusters based on what type of client can use the cluster. Possible values are NOTEBOOKS and JOBS.

items.docs: Returns information about all pinned and active clusters, and up to 200 of the most recently terminated all-purpose clusters in the past 30 days, and up to 30 of the most recently terminated job clusters in the past 30 days.

info.name: Azure Databricks Pin a Cluster

items.docs: Pins a cluster to ensure it is always returned by the list clusters API. Pinning a cluster that is already pinned has no effect.

info.name: Azure Databricks Unpin a Cluster

items.docs: Unpins a cluster. Unpinning a cluster that is not pinned has no effect.

info.name: Azure Databricks List Cluster Events

items.docs: Retrieves a list of events about the activity of a cluster. Events are returned in reverse chronological order. This endpoint allows paginating through cluster events using the next_page field.

info.name: Azure Databricks List Available Spark Versions

items.docs: Returns the list of available Databricks Runtime versions. These versions can be used to launch clusters.

info.name: Azure Databricks List Available Node Types

items.docs: Returns a list of supported Azure VM node types. These node types can be used to launch clusters.

info.name: Jobs
Azure Databricks Create a New Job

items.docs: Creates a new job with the provided settings. Returns the job_id of the newly created job.

info.name: Azure Databricks List All Jobs

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of jobs to return. Default is 20 and maximum is 25.

params.name: offset
params.description: Offset of the first job to return

params.name: name
params.description: A filter on the list based on the exact (case-insensitive) job name

params.name: expand_tasks
params.description: Whether to include task and cluster details in the response

items.docs: Retrieves a list of jobs defined in the workspace. Results are paginated with a default limit of 20 jobs per page.

info.name: Azure Databricks Get a Single Job

params.name: job_id
params.description: The canonical identifier of the job to retrieve

items.docs: Retrieves the details for a single job, including its settings and most recent run information.

info.name: Azure Databricks Partially Update a Job

items.docs: Adds, changes, or removes specific settings of an existing job. Use reset to overwrite all settings.

info.name: Azure Databricks Overwrite All Job Settings

items.docs: Overwrites all settings for a specific job. Use update to change individual settings.

info.name: Azure Databricks Delete a Job

items.docs: Deletes a job and sends an email to the addresses specified in email_notifications. No action occurs if the job has already been removed.

info.name: Azure Databricks Trigger a New Job Run

items.docs: Runs the job now and returns the run_id of the triggered run. A job can have at most one active run at a time unless max_concurrent_runs is set.

info.name: Azure Databricks Submit a One-time Run

items.docs: Submits a one-time run without creating a job. This endpoint allows you to submit a workload directly without defining a job. Returns the run_id of the submitted run.

info.name: Azure Databricks List Runs for a Job

params.name: job_id
params.description: Filter runs by the specified job ID

params.name: active_only
params.description: Show only active (running or pending) runs

params.name: completed_only
params.description: Show only completed runs

params.name: offset
params.description: Offset for pagination

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of runs to return. Maximum is 25.

params.name: run_type
params.description: Filter by run type

params.name: expand_tasks
params.description: Whether to include task details in the response

params.name: start_time_from
params.description: Filter runs starting after this timestamp (epoch ms)

params.name: start_time_to
params.description: Filter runs starting before this timestamp (epoch ms)

items.docs: Lists runs in descending order by start time for a specific job or all jobs.

info.name: Azure Databricks Get a Single Job Run

params.name: run_id
params.description: The canonical identifier of the run

params.name: include_history
params.description: Whether to include the repair history in the response

params.name: include_resolved_values
params.description: Whether to include resolved parameter values in the response

items.docs: Retrieves the metadata of a run including its status, timing, cluster information, and task details.

info.name: Azure Databricks Cancel a Job Run

items.docs: Cancels a run. The run is canceled asynchronously, so when this request completes, the run may still be running. The run is terminated shortly after the cancellation request.

info.name: Azure Databricks Delete a Job Run

items.docs: Deletes a non-active run. Returns an HTTP 400 error if the run is still active.

info.name: Azure Databricks Get Job Run Output

params.name: run_id
params.description: The canonical identifier of the run

items.docs: Retrieves the output and metadata of a single task run. When a notebook task returns a value through dbutils.notebook.exit(), this endpoint can be used to retrieve that value.

info.name: Workspace
Azure Databricks List Workspace Objects

params.name: path
params.description: The absolute path of the workspace directory to list. A path of / lists the root directory.

items.docs: Lists the contents of a directory in the workspace, or the object if it is not a directory. If the input path does not exist, this call returns an error RESOURCE_DOES_NOT_EXIST.

info.name: Azure Databricks Get Workspace Object Status

params.name: path
params.description: The absolute path of the workspace object

items.docs: Gets the status of an object or a directory. If the object is a directory, its contents are not included in the response.

info.name: Azure Databricks Create a Directory

items.docs: Creates the specified directory and all necessary parent directories if they do not exist. If there is an object (not a directory) at any prefix of the input path, this call returns an error RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS.

info.name: Azure Databricks Delete a Workspace Object

items.docs: Deletes an object or a directory (and optionally its contents recursively). If the path does not exist, this call returns an error RESOURCE_DOES_NOT_EXIST. If path is a non-empty directory and recursive is set to false, this call returns an error DIRECTORY_NOT_EMPTY.

info.name: Azure Databricks Import a Workspace Object

items.docs: Imports a notebook or the contents of an entire directory. If the path already exists and overwrite is set to false, this call returns an error RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS. Content can be provided inline as base64-encoded bytes or from a file.

info.name: Azure Databricks Export a Workspace Object

params.name: path
params.description: The absolute path of the object to export

params.name: format
params.description: Format of the exported content

params.name: direct_download
params.description: Whether to download the exported file directly. Default is false.

items.docs: Exports a notebook or the contents of an entire directory. The notebook is exported in the requested format (default is SOURCE). A directory is always exported as a DBC archive.
